title: Mind the Gap: Assessing Temporal Generalization in Neural Language Models

structured.summary: The paper investigates the temporal generalization capabilities of neural language models, highlighting that current static evaluation methods may not accurately reflect model performance on future data. It proposes time-stratified approaches to improve model adaptability and temporal evaluation benchmarks. Results show transformer models like Transformer-XL perform worse as the temporal gap from the training period increases.

strengths.point: Addresses critical gap in temporal generalization.
strengths.evidence: Paper demonstrates Transformer-XL models perform up to 16% worse when predicting data from beyond their training period.

strengths.point: Introduces dynamic benchmarks for temporal evaluation.
strengths.evidence: The release of benchmarks for WMT and arXiv facilitates language model evaluation with temporal dynamics in mind.

weaknesses.point: Limited evaluation on diverse model architectures.
weaknesses.evidence: The research focuses primarily on Transformer-XL without comparing results across other models like BERT or GPT.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient clarity on dynamic evaluation methods.
weaknesses.evidence: Details on the implementation of continuous updates through gradient descent are brief and lack specifics.
Lacks detailed explanation regarding the compilation and preprocessing of the 'C USTOM N EWS' dataset.

questions.question: How do temporal generalization findings extend to other neural architectures beyond Transformer-XL?
questions.motivation: Understanding whether the identified temporal degradation issue applies to a broader range of models could impact the generalizability of the results.

critique_points.point_001: Limited evaluation on diverse model architectures.. The research focuses primarily on Transformer-XL without comparing results across other models like BERT or GPT
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient clarity on dynamic evaluation methods.. Details on the implementation of continuous updates through gradient descent are brief and lack specifics
critique_points.point_003: Unclear dataset preparation process.. Lacks detailed explanation regarding the compilation and preprocessing of the 'C USTOM N EWS' dataset
